days.title: Day 1: I want — искам + noun

grammar.title: искам + noun (I want something)
grammar.explanation: The verb искам means 'I want'. To ask for a thing, just say искам plus the noun: искам кафе (I want coffee), искам вода (I want water). Bulgarian has no noun cases, so the noun never changes its ending after искам. The pronoun аз (I) is optional because the verb ending already shows the subject. Add моля (please) to be polite, usually at the end: Кафе, моля.

reading.title: В кафенето
reading.text: — Искам кафе, моля. — Да. А ти? — Аз искам чай.
reading.translation: — I want coffee, please. — Yes. And you? — I want tea.

days.speakingPrompt: Imagine you are at a café. Order three different drinks or foods using 'Искам ..., моля.' Say each one aloud.

days.title: Day 2: I/you/he want — искам, искаш, иска

grammar.title: искам, искаш, иска (singular forms)
grammar.explanation: искам changes its ending with the person. аз искам (I want), ти искаш (you want, one person), той/тя иска (he/she wants). The noun after the verb still does not change. Use какво (what) to ask: Какво искаш? = What do you want? Answer with искам + noun.

reading.title: Какво искаш?
reading.text: — Какво искаш? — Искам супа и хляб. — А тя? — Тя иска месо.
reading.translation: — What do you want? — I want soup and bread. — And she? — She wants meat.

days.speakingPrompt: Ask a friend 'Какво искаш?' and then answer for yourself and for a third person (той/тя). Use сирене, супа, and месо.

days.title: Day 3: We/you/they want — искаме, искате, искат

grammar.title: искаме, искате, искат (plural forms)
grammar.explanation: The plural forms are ние искаме (we want), вие искате (you want — plural or polite), те искат (they want). Now you know all six forms. The preposition с (with) becomes със before words starting with с or з, e.g. кафе със захар (coffee with sugar). Prepositions, not case endings, show relations in Bulgarian.

reading.title: В ресторанта
reading.text: — Какво искате? — Ние искаме супа и хляб. — А те? — Те искат кафе със захар.
reading.translation: — What do you want? — We want soup and bread. — And they? — They want coffee with sugar.

days.speakingPrompt: Order for a group: say what 'we' (ние) want and what 'they' (те) want. Include one drink със захар.

days.title: Day 4: I like — обичам + noun

grammar.title: обичам + noun (I like / love something)
grammar.explanation: обичам means 'I like' or 'I love' and works like искам: обичам + noun. It conjugates the same way: обичам, обичаш, обича, обичаме, обичате, обичат. To make a yes/no question, put ли after the verb: Обичаш ли чай? = Do you like tea? Add много for emphasis, before the noun: Обичам много кафе (I like coffee a lot).

reading.title: Обичаш ли чай?
reading.text: — Обичаш ли чай? — Да, обичам много чай. А ти обичаш ли кафе? — Не, обичам мляко.
reading.translation: — Do you like tea? — Yes, I like tea a lot. And do you like coffee? — No, I like milk.

days.speakingPrompt: Ask 'Обичаш ли ...?' about three foods or drinks, then answer for yourself with да or не. Use много at least once.

days.title: Day 5: I want to eat — искам да ям

grammar.title: да + present (искам да ям)
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian has NO infinitive. To say 'I want to eat', you use искам + да + a present-tense verb: искам да ям. The verb after да must agree with the subject: искам да ям (I want to eat), искаш да ядеш (you want to eat), той иска да яде (he wants to eat). The same works with пия (drink): искам да пия вода.

examples.english: Do you want to drink tea?

reading.title: Гладен съм
reading.text: — Искам да ям сега. — Какво искаш да ядеш? — Искам да ям хляб със сирене и да пия вода.
reading.translation: — I want to eat now. — What do you want to eat? — I want to eat bread with cheese and to drink water.

days.speakingPrompt: Say what you want to eat and drink right now using 'искам да ям ...' and 'искам да пия ...'. Then ask a friend 'Какво искаш да ядеш?'

days.title: Day 6: You want to drink — искаш да пиеш

vocab.exampleEn: What do you want to drink?

grammar.title: да + present across all persons; за + meal
grammar.explanation: Now combine да + present in the plural too: искаме да ядем (we want to eat), искаме да пием (we want to drink). The preposition за means 'for' and is great with meals: за закуска (for breakfast), за обяд (for lunch). Adjectives agree with the noun: топъл чай (m), but тя обича топла супа (f) — for now just learn топъл with masculine nouns.

examples.russian: За закуска искам да ям хляб със сирене.
examples.english: For breakfast I want to eat bread with cheese.

examples.russian: Какво искаш да пиеш за обяд?
examples.english: What do you want to drink for lunch?

reading.title: За закуска
reading.text: — Какво искаш за закуска? — Искам да ям хляб със сирене и да пия топъл чай. — А за обяд? — За обяд искаме да ядем супа.
reading.translation: — What do you want for breakfast? — I want to eat bread with cheese and to drink hot tea. — And for lunch? — For lunch we want to eat soup.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your meals: say what you want to eat and drink за закуска and за обяд. Use искам да ... and at least one adjective (топъл).

days.title: Day 7: Review — Food & drink

grammar.title: Recap: искам / обичам + noun and да + present
grammar.explanation: This week you learned two key patterns. (1) искам / обичам + noun: искам кафе (I want coffee), обичам сирене (I like cheese) — the noun never changes, because Bulgarian has no cases. Both verbs conjugate the same: -ам, -аш, -а, -аме, -ате, -ат. (2) Since there is no infinitive, 'to do something' is да + present, agreeing with the subject: искам да ям, искаш да ядеш, искаме да пием. Make yes/no questions with ли after the verb (Обичаш ли чай?) and use prepositions със (with) and за (for) instead of case endings.

examples.english: Do you like coffee with sugar?

examples.russian: За обяд искаме да ядем супа и хляб.
examples.english: For lunch we want to eat soup and bread.

examples.russian: Тя иска да пие топъл чай сега.
examples.english: She wants to drink hot tea now.

reading.title: В кафенето (review)
reading.text: — Здравей! Какво искаш да ядеш? — Обичам сирене, искам хляб със сирене. — А за пиене? — Искам да пия топъл чай със захар, моля.
reading.translation: — Hi! What do you want to eat? — I like cheese, I want bread with cheese. — And to drink? — I want to drink hot tea with sugar, please.

days.speakingPrompt: Role-play ordering a full meal at a café: greet, say what you like (обичам), order food and a drink (искам ..., искам да ям ...), and ask your friend 'Какво искаш да пиеш?'
